metadata.seed: Yes. That's something that I get very anxious about, having to cross the bridge. I have to cross the Ben Franklin bridge and then get over the bridge that goes to the airport by the stadium. I think that's the Girard point bridge. Yeah. That's two levels there. I have to do that both ways.

patient.query: Yes. That's something that I get very anxious about, having to cross the bridge. I have to cross the Ben Franklin bridge and then get over the bridge that goes to the airport by the stadium. I think that's the Girard point bridge. Yeah. That's two levels there. I have to do that both ways.

it's difficult to cross two levels

patient.query: Yeah, crossing bridges definitely gets my heart racing. It’s like this overwhelming sense of dread comes over me. I guess it’s all the ‘what ifs’ that start to spiral in my mind. It feels like I can’t just enjoy the view, because I’m so focused on the fear of what might happen.

I can’t shake that feeling, even when I know most bridges are fine. It’s frustrating because I really want to be able to just drive over without feeling this way.

patient.query: Um, yeah, I guess one of the worst things I think about is panicking and not being able to control the car. Like, what if I just freeze up and can’t move? I know it sounds dramatic, but it feels so real in those moments.

What if I freeze up?
What if I cause an accident?
